channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Palestinians return to ruins and US troops land in Israel as ceasefire holds
item.description: GAZA CITY, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of displaced Palestinians returned to their Gaza neighborhoods Saturday, weaving through dust-shrouded streets as bulldozers clawed through the wreckage of two years of war and a ceasefire held in its second day. “Gaza is completely destroyed.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, SAM METZ, SARAH EL DEEB and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro-Palestinian protesters march to stadium ahead of World Cup qualifier between Norway and Israel
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — Pro-Palestinian protesters lit flares and waved flags as they marched to the stadium ahead of the World Cup qualifier between Norway and Israel on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jared Kushner reenters as key player in Middle East peace push
item.description: Jared Kushner has reentered the political spotlight as a top envoy for the Trump White House in efforts to secure a peace deal in the Middle East, highlighting both his influence in the region and his growing business portfolio there.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: North Korea displays new long-range missile at a military parade celebrating Kim Jong Un
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — At a massive military parade attended by foreign leaders, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un rolled out his nuclear-armed military’s most powerful weapons, including a new intercontinental ballistic missile he may be preparing to test in coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Drone footage above Gaza City shows the toll of 2 years of war
item.description: GAZA CITY, Gaza Strip (AP) — New drone footage shows what remains of Gaza City after two years of war and as a ceasefire holds in its second day. The footage taken by The Associated Press on Saturday shows few buildings still standing in the Tal al-Hawa neighborhood. The rest appear to be gutted.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelensky says Gaza peace deal shows promise for Ukraine after call with Trump
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Saturday said President Trump’s ability to successfully broker a peace agreement between Israel and Hamas provides hope for an end to the war in Eastern Europe.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump peace plan for Gaza could be just a 'pause' before Hamas strikes again, experts warn
item.description: Regional coordination supports President Donald Trump's Gaza peace plan, but analysts caution that Hamas's continued existence could reignite conflict.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: France's re-appointed prime minister Lecornu calls for calm amid political chaos
item.description: France’s newly re-appointed Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu acknowledged that he might be able to stay in his job long given the country's deep political divides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Two powerful offshore earthquakes struck the same region in the southern Philippines hours apart on Friday with the first 7.4 magnitude temblor killing at least seven people, setting off landslides and prompting evacuations of coastal areas nearby because of a brief tsunam
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Power restored to 800,000 in Kyiv after major Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy grid
item.description: Power has been restored to over 800,000 residents in Kyiv, a day after Russia launched major attacks on Ukraine's power grid, causing widespread blackouts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel rejects freeing from prison the most popular Palestinian leader
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The most popular and potentially unifying Palestinian leader — Marwan Barghouti — is not among the prisoners Israel intends to free in exchange for hostages held by Hamas under the new Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH, JULIA FRANKEL and JALAL BWAITAL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Latest: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza return north as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza were starting to walk north to return to their homes Friday, after the Israeli military said the ceasefire agreement with Hamas came into effect at noon local time and that its soldiers were withdrawing to the agreed-upon deployment lines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to visit Egypt and Israel early next week amid ceasefire deal in Middle East
item.description: President Trump said Friday he plans to visit Egypt and Israel during a one-day trip to the Middle East early next week to mark the implementation of a peace agreement that will see a pause in fighting in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Putin praises Trump's peace efforts as 'really doing a lot' to resolve global crises and conflicts
item.description: President Donald Trump's efforts to negotiate peace deals, including the Israel-Hamas truce, received praise from Russian President Vladimir Putin at a summit in Tajikistan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US sends CENTCOM troops to Israel for Trump-brokered Hamas ceasefire monitoring
item.description: Adm. Brad Cooper said U.S. Central Command troops will establish a ceasefire monitoring center in Israel as the President Donald Trump-brokered peace deal between Israel and Hamas begins.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize bets on Polymarket raise leak concerns, prompts probe
item.description: The last-minute bets, first reported by local outlets, are a cause for concern for the Norwegian officials in charge of awarding the honor
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Putin praises Trump's peace efforts despite failing to win the Nobel prize
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Friday praised U.S. President Donald Trump's peace efforts, despite not winning a Nobel, and voiced hope for securing an agreement to extend a pivotal nuclear arms pact with Washington for another year.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize chair responds to Trump's pressure campaign
item.description: The chair of the Nobel Peace Prize committee responded Friday to President Trump's public pressure campaign to win the award, telling reporters that the deliberation process for choosing the awardee prioritizes courage and integrity.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Israel's military says Gaza ceasefire now in effect
item.description: Israel's military announced early Friday that the ceasefire in Gaza is now in effect, after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's Cabinet approved the first phase of President Trump's 20-point peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: King Mohammed VI set to speak as Gen Z protesters demand reforms in Morocco
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco's elusive King Mohammed VI inaugurates parliament Friday in a speech with far higher stakes than in recent years, as anti-government protests sweep the nation's cities, questioning the government's spending priorities.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: Philippine seismologists say that a new offshore earthquake with a preliminary 6.8 magnitude hit the same southern region struck earlier by an earthquake with a magnitude of 7.4.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: From grief to gratitude: Trump praised as Israelis celebrate Hamas hostage deal in Tel Aviv
item.description: Israelis express joy, relief and grief at Hostages Square following President Donald Trump's announcement of agreement to end Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Farm workers forced into slavery, Greek police say
item.description: ATHENS, Oct 9 (REUTERS) - Greece has dismantled a criminal ring which trafficked dozens of Nepali men, beat them and forced them to work in farms across the country in degrading conditions with little or no payment, police said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Yannis Souliotis and Renee Maltezou, NewsNation

item.title: Trump peace deal triggers 72-hour countdown for Hamas to release 48 hostages from Gaza
item.description: Israeli security cabinet will convene Thursday to approve the Hamas hostage deal, starting a 96-hour countdown for the release of 48 hostages held for over two years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tropical Storm Jerry churns in Atlantic as weakened Priscilla nears Mexico
item.description: Priscilla lost its hurricane status and was downgraded to a tropical storm as it churned up Mexico's western Pacific coast.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pushing for drastic reduction in refugees allowed into US
item.description: "Our concern is that this could turn what has long been a globally responsive humanitarian system into one that overwhelmingly favors a single group.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: US diplomat fired over relationship with woman accused of Chinese Communist Party ties
item.description: The dismissal is believed to be the first of its kind for violating a ban on such relationships that was introduced late last year under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel seizes another Gaza flotilla
item.description: Israel intercepted another flotilla attempting to break the naval blockade and reach Gaza. This comes just days after another fleet was intercepted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deadline for Mexico to pay water owed to US fast approaching
item.description: The clock is ticking down on Mexico's deadline this month to pay the United States water it owes under a 1944 international treaty.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Priscilla nears major hurricane status in Pacific as tropical storm swirls in Atlantic
item.description: Just off the west coast of Mexico, Priscilla was spinning with maximum sustained winds around 105 mph and moving northwest at 9 mph.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Hamas Oct 7 attacks survivor recalls Gaza captivity 2 years later
item.description: Two years after the Oct. 7 Hamas attack on Israel, survivor Aviva Siegel recounts the torture and starvation she endured during her Gaza captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rare ancient artwork vanishes from tomb in Egypt: Report
item.description: The artwork, a 4,000-year-old limestone relief, was reported to have disappeared over the weekend from a Fifth Dynasty tomb.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Greta Thunberg is among flotilla activists deported from Israel. Others remain in prison
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli authorities said Monday they deported to Greece and Slovakia another 171 people detained for taking part in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla, including prominent Swedish activist Greta Thunberg.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and RENATA BRITO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Snowstorm traps hundreds of hikers on Mount Everest during China's national holiday
item.description: About 350 hikers had reached a meeting point in Tingri country and rescuers were in contact with another 200, state broadcaster CCTV said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Israeli and Hamas officials discuss US peace plan for Gaza
item.description: Israeli and Hamas officials are holding indirect talks in an Egyptian resort Monday on a U.S. peace plan for Gaza.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Japan’s ruling party elects Sanae Takaichi as new leader, likely to become first female PM
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s governing party on Saturday elected former Economic Security Minister Sanae Takaichi, a hard-line ultra-conservative and China hawk, as its new leader, making her likely to become the country’s first female prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UK police given more time to question 4 suspects over deadly Manchester synagogue attack
item.description: LONDON (AP) — U.K. police were given extra time Saturday to question four people arrested on suspicion of terror offenses after an attack on a synagogue in northwest England that left two people dead and Britain’s Jewish community shocked and grieving.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation
